title: Cisco Wireless LAN Configuration
description: Configure autonomous access point SSIDs, WPA2 security, radio settings, and verification commands for enterprise wireless LAN deployment.

tasks.prompt: Enter Global Configuration mode on autonomous AP AP1 to begin wireless configuration.
tasks.explanation: Autonomous access points run Cisco IOS and require global configuration mode to define SSIDs and radio parameters.

tasks.prompt: Create a global SSID profile named 'CORP-WIFI'.
tasks.explanation: The 'dot11 ssid' command creates a wireless network identifier (SSID) profile that can be assigned to radio interfaces.

tasks.prompt: Map SSID 'CORP-WIFI' to VLAN 100 for client traffic segmentation.
tasks.explanation: Assigning a VLAN to an SSID tags wireless client traffic so the upstream switch can place it in the correct broadcast domain.

tasks.prompt: Enable open authentication on SSID 'CORP-WIFI' (required before WPA2 key management).
tasks.explanation: Open authentication allows clients to associate with the SSID; WPA2-PSK provides the actual encryption and access control.

tasks.prompt: Enable WPA version 2 key management on SSID 'CORP-WIFI'.
tasks.explanation: WPA2 (802.11i) uses AES-CCMP encryption. The 'version 2' keyword selects WPA2 over legacy WPA.

tasks.prompt: Set the WPA2 pre-shared key to 'CiscoSecure2024!' on SSID 'CORP-WIFI'.
tasks.explanation: The PSK is the passphrase clients use to derive encryption keys. The 'ascii 0' keyword stores the key in cleartext in the running config.

tasks.prompt: Enable guest mode on SSID 'CORP-WIFI' so the AP broadcasts the network name.
tasks.explanation: Guest mode makes the SSID visible in client wireless scans. Without it, the network is hidden and clients must know the exact SSID.

tasks.prompt: Enter configuration mode for the 2.4 GHz radio interface.
tasks.explanation: dot11Radio 0 is the 2.4 GHz radio on most autonomous APs. SSIDs and channel settings are applied per radio interface.

tasks.prompt: Bind SSID 'CORP-WIFI' to the 2.4 GHz radio interface.
tasks.explanation: An SSID must be assigned to a radio interface before clients can associate. Multiple SSIDs can be broadcast from a single radio.

tasks.prompt: Set the 2.4 GHz radio to use channel 6 to reduce co-channel interference.
tasks.explanation: In the 2.4 GHz band, channels 1, 6, and 11 are non-overlapping. Manual channel assignment helps in dense AP deployments.

tasks.prompt: Set the local transmit power level to 17 dBm on the 2.4 GHz radio.
tasks.explanation: Adjusting transmit power controls cell size and reduces co-channel interference between adjacent access points.

tasks.prompt: Display all wireless clients currently associated with this access point.
tasks.explanation: This command lists associated clients with MAC address, SSID, signal strength, and VLAN—essential for wireless troubleshooting.
